answer.answer: Serious factory accidents must be reported to the factory inspector within 12 hours [Source 1].

sources.excerpt: reported to the factory inspector within 12 hours

answer.answer: OISD-130 requires certified equipment for hazardous zones, lockout/tagout procedures, quarterly transformer oil testing, annual switchgear/grounding inspections, semi-annual circuit breaker tests, documented emergency shutdowns, arc flash analysis, and posted PPE requirements [Source 1].

sources.excerpt: electrical equipment in hazardous areas must be certified

answer.answer: Equipment tagged EQ-1001 through EQ-4002, transformers (oil testing), and compressors COMP-C01/COMP-C02 (bearing inspection) require quarterly inspections. [Source 1] [Source 2] [Source 3]

sources.excerpt: eq - 1001 through eq - 4002 quarterly
transformer oil testing quarterly
compressors comp - c01 comp - c02 quarterly bearing

answer.answer: TNK-T01 and TNK-T02 require annual internal inspection per OISD-116 and safety manual; as Group A tanks they also need biennial external inspection per OISD-117.

sources.excerpt: annual internal inspection
annual internal inspection
biennial external inspection

answer.answer: Hot work permits are mandatory for any work involving open flames, sparks, or temperatures exceeding 30°C above ambient in hazardous areas. [Source 1]

sources.excerpt: hot work permits are mandatory for any work involving open flames, sparks, or temperatures exceeding 30°c above ambient in hazardous areas.

answer.answer: OISD-116 requires a minimum of 2 emergency response teams per shift [Source 1].

sources.excerpt: minimum of 2 emergency response teams per shift

answer.answer: Mining workers must wear hard hats, safety boots, high-visibility vests, safety glasses, respiratory protection in dusty areas, and hearing protection above 85 dB [Source 1]. Factories handling hazardous processes must provide adequate and suitable PPE for all workers [Source 3].

sources.excerpt: all workers entering mining areas must wear mandatory ppe
adequate and suitable ppe for all workers

answer.answer: A factory with 1000 or more workers must appoint a safety officer [Source 1].

sources.excerpt: every factory having 1000 or more

answer.answer: The minimum fresh air supply requirement for underground mines is 0.1 m³/s per worker. [Source 1]

sources.excerpt: fresh air supply must be maintained at a minimum of 0.1 m³/s per worker

answer.answer: Fire drills must be conducted at least once every quarter in factories. [Source 1]

sources.excerpt: fire drills must be conducted at least once every quarter

answer.answer: High-pressure piping (>15 bar) requires annual thickness measurement per OISD-119. [Source 1]

sources.excerpt: high-pressure piping (>15 bar) requires annual thickness measurement

answer.answer: Workers have the right to be informed about hazards, receive safety training, report unsafe conditions without retaliation, refuse work in immediate danger, and form safety committees with quarterly meetings [Source 1].

sources.excerpt: workers have the right to be informed

key_points: Right to hazard info and training

answer.answer: Section 38 requires fencing of all moving parts including flywheels, gears, pulleys, parts in motion during operation, and fixed parts that may move to create danger [Source 1].

sources.excerpt: all moving parts including flywheels, gears, and pulleys

answer.answer: Gas monitoring in underground mines requires continuous methane and CO monitoring, checks at the working face every 2 hours per DGMS-2022-05, and daily calibration of gas monitoring equipment per DGMS-2023-01.

sources.excerpt: methane and carbon monoxide
gas monitoring equipment must be checked daily

answer.answer: New mining workers must complete 40 hours of safety induction training, with refresher training every 6 months and monthly emergency response drills [Source 1].

sources.excerpt: 40 hours safety induction, refresher every 6 months, monthly drills

answer.answer: Group A tanks (hazardous materials) require annual internal and biennial external inspections, while Group B tanks (flammable liquids) require biennial internal and annual external inspections [Source 1]. All tanks require quarterly foundation visual inspections and ultrasonic bottom scanning every 5 years [Source 1].

sources.excerpt: Group A tanks: annual internal inspection, biennial external inspection
Group B tanks: biennial internal inspection, annual external inspection

answer.answer: HAZOP studies are required for all new installations and modifications, must be revalidated every 5 years, and all action items must be tracked to completion [Source 1].

sources.excerpt: hazop studies are required for all new installations and modifications to existing units

key_points: Required for new installations and modifications

answer.answer: Safety measures for hazardous processes include providing adequate PPE, medical examinations, safety showers, eye wash stations, worker training on hazardous material handling, emergency plans, and regular exposure monitoring. [Source 1]

sources.excerpt: factories must provide the following
